attributes.description:
By default, form controls are associated with the nearest containing `<form>` element. This attribute allows you
to place the form control outside of a form and associate it with the form that has this `id`. The form must be in
the same document or shadow root for this to work.
attributes.name: form

attributes.description: The radio groups's help text. If you need to display HTML, use the `help-text` slot instead.
attributes.name: help-text

attributes.description:
The radio group's label. Required for proper accessibility. If you need to display HTML, use the `label` slot
instead.
attributes.name: label

attributes.description: The name of the radio group, submitted as a name/value pair with form data.
attributes.name: name

attributes.description: Ensures a child radio is checked before allowing the containing form to submit.
attributes.name: required

attributes.description: The radio group's size. This size will be applied to all child radios and radio buttons.
attributes.name: size

attributes.description: The current value of the radio group, submitted as a name/value pair with form data.
attributes.name: value

cssParts.description: The button group that wraps radio buttons.
cssParts.name: button-group

cssParts.description: The form control that wraps the label, input, and help text.
cssParts.name: form-control

events.description: Emitted when the radio group's selected value changes.
events.name: syn-change

events.description: Emitted when the radio group receives user input.
events.name: syn-input

events.description: Emitted when the form control has been checked for validity and its constraints aren't satisfied.
events.name: syn-invalid

methods.description: Checks for validity but does not show a validation message. Returns `true` when valid and `false` when invalid.
methods.name: checkValidity

methods.description: Gets the associated form, if one exists.
methods.name: getForm

methods.description: Checks for validity and shows the browser's validation message if the control is invalid.
methods.name: reportValidity

methods.description: Sets a custom validation message. Pass an empty string to restore validity.
methods.name: setCustomValidity

properties.description:
By default, form controls are associated with the nearest containing `<form>` element. This attribute allows you
to place the form control outside of a form and associate it with the form that has this `id`. The form must be in
the same document or shadow root for this to work.
properties.name: form

properties.description: The radio groups's help text. If you need to display HTML, use the `help-text` slot instead.
properties.name: helpText

properties.description:
The radio group's label. Required for proper accessibility. If you need to display HTML, use the `label` slot
instead.
properties.name: label

properties.description: The name of the radio group, submitted as a name/value pair with form data.
properties.name: name

properties.description: Ensures a child radio is checked before allowing the containing form to submit.
properties.name: required

properties.description: The radio group's size. This size will be applied to all child radios and radio buttons.
properties.name: size

properties.description: The current value of the radio group, submitted as a name/value pair with form data.
properties.name: value

slots.description: The default slot where `<syn-radio>` or `<syn-radio-button>` elements are placed.
The radio group's label. Required for proper accessibility. Alternatively, you can use the `label` attribute.
slots.name: label

slots.description: Text that describes how to use the radio group. Alternatively, you can use the `help-text` attribute.
slots.name: help-text

summary: Radio groups are used to group multiple [radios](/components/radio) or [radio buttons](/components/radio-button) so they function as a single form control.
